Opportunity: Economic growth drives demand, fosters innovation, and promotes expansion, creating opportunities for enhanced revenue, greater market share, ESG initiatives, and sustainable success.
Appropriate Steps are taken to mitigate risk.

Risk: Ethical lapses, integrity breaches and poor governance can lead to reputational damage, legal liabilities and loss of stakeholder trust, jeopardizing Company’s standing, relationships and long-term viability.
Opportunity: Commitment to ethics, integrity and strong governance practices enhances trust, attracts investors, and fosters  positive corporate culture, driving sustainable growth, innovation and competitive advantage while ensuring long-term stakeholder value.

Addressing risks related to ethics, integrity, and governance requires development of strong policies and frameworks,  promotion of  culture centered on accountability and transparency, execution of regular audits,  introduction of ethical training initiatives,  enhancement of whistleblower protections, and  assurance of rigorous adherence to legal and regulatory requirements.
Community development initiatives enables Company to make a positive social impact, strengthen relationships, and enhance reputation. Investing in various domain fosters inclusive growth.
Appropriate Steps are taken to mitigate risk

GHG emissions pose significant Environmental Risks, contributing to climate change, extreme weather events, and resource scarcity. Failure to mitigate emissions can result in regulatory penalties, supply chain disruptions and reputational damage, exposing Company to financial losses and operational challenges.

Mitigating greenhouse gas emissions requiresadoption of renewable energy sources, improvements in energy efficiency, investments in green technologies,  implementation of carbon offset programs, regular assessments of emissions, compliance with regulatory standards, promotion of sustainable practices, and active involvement of stakeholders in climate-aware initiatives.

Risk: Poor waste management practices can result in environmental pollution, non-compliance with regulations, and health risks for local communities. Improper disposal may lead to legal liabilities, financial penalties, and reputational harm, while inefficient waste handling can drive up operational costs and lead to resource inefficiencies.
Opportunity: Implementing effective waste management strategies offers significant opportunities for cost savings, resource recovery, and environmental responsibility. Through practices such as recycling, reusing, and reducing waste, the company can lower its environmental footprint, enhance operational efficiency, and strengthen its brand reputation.

Strategies for waste management mitigation encompass adoption of sustainable disposal techniques,  implementation of waste reduction and recycling initiatives, adherence to environmental regulations, investment in cutting-edge waste management technologies, regular auditing processes, promotion of awareness campaigns, and  optimization of resource use to enhance efficiency.

Supply chain disruptions caused by events like natural disasters, geopolitical conflicts, or supplier failures can result in inventory shortages, production delays, and revenue loss. A lack of visibility and resilience within  supply chain can further increase costs, damage  company's reputation, and lead to a loss of market share.

Addressing supply chain risks requires a multifaceted approach that includes diversifying suppliers, improving inventory management practices, establishing comprehensive risk assessment frameworks, utilizing technology for real-time monitoring, nurturing strong relationships with suppliers, creating contingency plans, ensuring adherence to regulatory standards, and investing in initiatives that enhance supply chain resilience.
Failure to prioritize employees’ Health and Safety can result in workplace accidents, injuries and legal liabilities, damaging Morale, Productivity and Company’s Reputation.

Measures to enhance employee health and safety include  establishment of rigorous safety protocols,  organization of frequent training programs,  provision of personal protective equipment, adherence to health regulations,  promotion of a culture focused on safety, the execution of regular risk assessments, and creation of effective incident reporting and response systems.

Maintaining high product quality boosts customer satisfaction, strengthens loyalty, and enhances brand reputation. This, in turn, drives competitive advantage, sets the company apart in the market, and supports long-term profitability.
Appropriate Steps are taken to mitigate risk.

Effective human resource management practices such as talent acquisition, development, and retention play a key role in enhancing employee engagement, boosting productivity, and driving organizational success. These efforts help build a positive work culture and provide a competitive advantage.
Appropriate Steps are taken to mitigate risk.

Investing in the development of employees’ skills, knowledge, and capabilities enhances workforce performance, fosters innovation, and increases adaptability. This empowers the company to achieve its strategic goals and sustain long-term growth.
Appropriate Steps are taken to mitigate risk.

Building strong Customer relationships through personalized experiences, responsive support and consistent communication drives Customer loyalty, retention and advocacy, leading to increased sales, market share and profitability.
Appropriate Steps are taken to mitigate risk.

Weak corporate governance practices, including a lack of transparency, accountability, and ethical standards, can result in conflicts of interest, regulatory breaches, and financial misconduct. These issues can erode investor confidence and threaten the company's overall stability.

Mitigating corporate governance risk involves  creation of strong governance structures, promoting transparency, enforcing accountability protocols, upholding ethical standards, performing regular audits, instituting whistleblower protections, ensuring regulatory compliance, and facilitating effective communication with stakeholders and investors.

Involvement in anti-competitive practices or corrupt activities, such as price-fixing or bribery, can subject companies to legal penalties, regulatory sanctions, and reputational harm. Such actions undermine market competitiveness and erode stakeholder trust.

Strategies to mitigate anti-competitive practices and corruption involve development of thorough compliance programs,  enforcement of stringent ethical standards,  provision of regular training, the implementation of effective monitoring systems,  promotion of transparency, adherence to competition regulations,  establishment of whistleblower mechanisms, and  cultivation of a culture of integrity.

Insufficient whistle-blower protection or ineffective response mechanisms can discourage reporting of unethical or illegal activities, allowing misconduct to go unaddressed. This can lead to legal liabilities, regulatory investigations, and damage to the company’s reputation.

Measures to support whistleblower initiatives involve  creation of strong protective policies, safeguarding confidentiality, enhancing awareness programs, establishing efficient reporting systems, carrying out unbiased investigations, responding swiftly to reported concerns, monitoring potential for retaliation, and cultivating an environment of trust, ethical behaviour, and transparency

At IFGL, we have established clear commitments across Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) spectrum, each with defined objectives and timelines to ensure accountability and track progress. These ESG commitments are deeply embedded in our core values and are reflected in our daily operations. 
Environmental Sustainability is a key focus area, where we are dedicated to pollution prevention and control. This includes identifying potential pollution risks associated with our activities and implementing effective measures to mitigate them. We promote circularity through initiatives aimed at reducing waste and enhancing recycling efforts. Additionally, we are committed to conserving natural resources by adopting sustainable practices that minimize our environmental footprint. 
On the social front, we are committed to maintaining a safe and healthy work environment, guided by our “Zero Harm Vision,” which encompasses safety for life, environment, and property. We continuously strive to improve product quality and workplace conditions, ensuring well-being of our employees. Our active participation in Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives reflects our commitment to supporting and uplifting communities in which we operate. 
In terms of governance, we uphold strict compliance with legal and regulatory frameworks set by State and Central Authorities. We are focused on building a strong governance framework that upholds ethical conduct and transparency across all regions where we conduct business.

At IFGL,  we recognize critical importance of sustainability and are fully committed to embedding it across every aspect of our operations. Sustainability is embraced as a core value, and we have taken proactive steps to develop and implement a comprehensive Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) strategy. Our overarching objective is to make sustainability central to our business, driving long-term success and delivering a positive impact. Looking ahead, we remain focused on advancing sustainable practices that foster innovation and growth, setting a benchmark for sustainability within our industry, the Company is operating.
We have introduced several initiatives focused on water conservation, energy efficiency, waste reduction, recycling, and  reduction of greenhouse gas emissions. Our Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) programs are dedicated to uplifting underprivileged communities near our manufacturing sites, addressing key areas such as health, education, hygiene, sports, skills development, and overall well-being.
Governance is a cornerstone of our operations, with ethical practices firmly ingrained in our day-to-day business conduct. These initiatives highlight our deep commitment to both environmental sustainability and social responsibility, as we strive to make a meaningful difference in lives of those around us.
As we move forward, our unwavering focus on sustainability will continue to guide our actions. We understand that this is an evolving journey and remain committed to continuous improvement and innovation in this space. By placing sustainability at the heart of our business, we aim to deliver long-term value to our stakeholders while positively contributing to the communities we serve.
We are equally committed to fostering collaboration and building strong partnerships to tackle sustainability challenges together, paving the way for a brighter and more sustainable future. We view sustainability as a powerful opportunity to drive positive change and enhance transparency. Through innovation, collaboration, and a strong alignment with our core values, we are confident in our ability to create a lasting impact and build a better tomorrow for all.

S No 1(b) - All Company policies are approved by the Board, its Committees, or authorized individuals, depending on the nature and scope of the policy.

S No. 2 - The Company has translated its policies into actionable procedures and implemented them across various levels of operations. This is carried out through designated committees and/or responsible personnel tasked with ensuring the effective execution of the respective policies and procedures.

S No 3 - The Company has adopted transparent business practices as a core value and communicates these principles to its value chain partners, including suppliers and logistics service providers, as deemed necessary. 


S No. 11 - Assessments and evaluations of policies concerning Health, Safety, Environment, and Governance are primarily conducted internally on a periodic basis by the Company. If necessary, the Company shall have evaluation done by engaging an external agency. 


S No. 10 - The Company's performance is regularly reviewed by the Board, its Committees, and other responsible authorities. Any deviation is promptly addressed and rectified.
The Company has allocated dedicated resources to ensure regulatory compliance and has established a strong process for identifying and addressing any instances of non-compliance. Compliances status is regularly reviewed and communicated to the Board, its Committees, and other relevant stakeholders.

IFGL organized training sessions for its directors covering various ESG aspects, including existing policies and procedures.   To reinforce leadership commitment and enhance  ESG performance of the organization, additional training was conducted for Senior Management, focusing on key material issues relevant to the Company's business operations.

ESG training programs for Key Managerial Personnel have significantly contributed to strengthening governance, enhancing decision-making, managing risks more effectively, fostering ethical leadership, and boosting overall organizational performance. The Company organised:
(a) ESG awareness programs 
(b) .Internal audit training focused on ISO standards:
• ISO 9001:2015 – Quality Management System
• ISO 14001:2015 – Environmental Management System
• ISO 45001:2018 – Occupational Health and Safety Management System
(c) Awareness training on ISO 31000:2018 – Risk Management Framework

IFGL believes that a well-trained workforce is essential for fulfilling roles and responsibilities efficiently and contributing meaningfully to the organization’s success. To support this, the Company has implemented 5S tools across its operations and provided employee training to ensure its effective execution. Additionally, IFGL has conducted training programs focused on health and safety, employee well-being, and other relevant areas to foster a safe and supportive work environment.

IFGL recognizes employee training as a vital investment to drive productivity, enhance quality, foster innovation, and improve customer satisfaction. As part of this commitment, the Company monthly conducted mandatory Occupational Health and Safety training for workers.

IFGL recognizes that strong Anti-Corruption and Anti-Bribery measures are crucial for maintaining ethical standards, ensuring legal and regulatory compliance, and fostering trust among stakeholders. The Company has established a comprehensive Anti-Corruption and Anti-Bribery (ABAC) Policy and Procedure applicable to all employees and business associates. This policy is also communicated to external stakeholders, including vendors, suppliers, and service providers.
The primary objective of ABAC policy is to promote honest and ethical business practices. IFGL has adopted a zero-tolerance approach to bribery and corruption and is committed to conducting its business with professionalism, fairness, and integrity. ABAC policy outlines the process for conducting risk assessments, raising concerns, and the procedural flow for investigating ABAC-related issues

IFGL has established a comprehensive 'Code of Conduct' for its Board of Directors, aimed at preventing and effectively managing conflicts of interest related to its business operations. As a safeguard, the Company obtains annual declarations from Board members to confirm absence of any such conflicts.
Key aspects of the Code of Conduct include:

• Acceptance of Payments or Gifts: Under no circumstances should any individual accept offers, payments,   promises to pay, or authorizations to pay money or anything of value from customers or vendors that could be perceived, —directly or indirectly,,— as influencing business decisions or facilitating fraudulent activity.
•Corporate Opportunities: Individuals must not exploit business opportunities discovered using corporate resources for personal gain.

IFGL is committed to taking appropriate action in the event of any violation of the Code of Conduct. The Company has also outlined clear, transparent guidelines for professional behavior, including  specific roles and responsibilities of Independent Directors in identifying and managing conflicts of interest.

Leadership Indicator:

Sl No. 1 - IFGL has conducted training program for Value Chain Partners, including Suppliers, Distributors and Other Business, essential for ensuring quality of Raw Materials, compliance as per applicable regulatory regime and efficiency throughout Value Chain of the Company. The Company conducted capacity building workshops and training program for its Value Chain partners to educate and create awareness on ESG issues that are material to its Business Operation viz. Human Rights, Labor Welfare, Occupational Health & Safety and regulatory compliance. The Company also conducted training program for its Value Chain Partners on three important ESG aspects namely Sustainable Supply Chain and Responsible Sourcing, Occupational Health and Safety and ESG Policies.

Sl no. 1 - IFGL has undertaken several qualitative initiatives to enhance product quality, environmental performance, and sustainability across its operations. A dedicated Research and Development (R&D) facility has been established to ensure rigorous quality control and optimal performance of both finished products and input materials. At the Visakhapatnam plant, process stack heights have been elevated above surrounding structures to support effective dispersion and compliance with air quality standards. Capital investments have been directed toward pollution prevention and control systems to maintain healthy ambient air conditions. In line with its sustainability goals, the company has prohibited the use of plastic drinking water bottles across all locations and has adopted wastepaper-based mold making for packaging applications. Additionally, significant investment has been made in solar energy infrastructure at the Visakhapatnam manufacturing facility to promote clean energy generation and reduce dependence on conventional power sources. -FY 24-25 During the year,  the Company incurred Capital Expenditure of Rs  0.39 crores for the Research Centre.
FY 23-24 -The Company incurred Capital Expenditure of Rs  18.42 crores for the Research Centre.


SL No. 2a - Yes, IFGL adheres to procedures for Sustainable Sourcing, which have been formulated and executed in accordance with ISO 20400:2017. Prior to onboarding, all new Supply Chain Partners are required to undergo a thorough evaluation of essential ESG material factors, including Fair Business Practices, Human Rights and Labor, Occupational Health and Safety (H&S), Environmental Protection, Quality Management, and Risk Management. IFGL actively collaborates with its Suppliers to fully convey its vision and objectives regarding sustainable policies and goals feasible. 

Sl No. 4 -The Company use plastic for packaging its products and is therefore classified as a ‘Brand Owner’ under the Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2016, and the Guidelines on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR), 2022. In alignment with these regulations, the Company is actively managing its plastic waste to the extent possible.

The Company has established  comprehensive seven-stage Grievance Redressal Mechanism, outlining clear objectives, commitments, and defined roles and responsibilities for both site-level and corporate-level employees and workers. IFGL’s workforce is encouraged to raise concerns through appropriate channels, including department heads, immediate supervisors, or directly with  Human Resources (HR) Department. 
A well-structured communication system is in place to facilitate grievance reporting. Employees and workers can voice their concerns using a grievance form, via email, or by phone, including direct access to the Audit Committee Chairman and its members. The Company is committed to resolving grievances promptly and efficiently. 
Additionally, IFGL has constituted plantplant-level grievance committees  to address location-specific concerns. These committees focus on issues related to health and safety, prevention of sexual harassment, and labour management.

Non-permanent employees and workers can voice their grievances through their respective supervisors. Any instances of unethical behaviour, workplace misconduct, or violations can also be reported via the Whistle Blower Mechanism. These concerns are then escalated to the appropriate personnel to ensure timely and effective resolution.

IFGL has fostered a strong safety culture by embedding health and safety considerations into its business planning, decision-making, and management practices. The Company has translated its core health and safety values into actionable objectives at the operational level, focusing on ensuring employee well-being, maintaining a safe work environment, and preventing workplace incidents and injuries. 
To support this commitment, IFGL has implemented an integrated Quality, Environmental, and Health & Safety Management System across its manufacturing facilities in India. These systems are aligned with internationally recognized standards—ISO 9001:2015 (Quality Management), ISO 14001:2015 (Environmental Management), and ISO 45001:2018 (Occupational Health and Safety Management).

IFGL places a strong emphasis on health and safety and has implemented  following measures to uphold  safe working environment:

•A five-stage systematic risk management process  adopted to identify potential hazards and risks across its manufacturing facilities in India. Internal stakeholders, including plant heads and EHS team members, play  key role in identifying workplace hazards and managing associated risks.

•Safe work procedures  developed and implemented for both routine and non-routine operational activities.

•Regular internal and external audits  conducted, and workers  encouraged to report any unsafe conditions or practices observed within the manufacturing facilities.

•Periodic safety inspections  carried out to identify work-related hazards and evaluate risks associated with daily activities. Findings from these inspections  regularly communicated to senior management.

•ImplementedI a cloud-based safety management system and equipped relevant personnel at manufacturing plants with a mobile application to facilitate real-time reporting and tracking of near misses and workplace incidents.

IFGL places the highest priority on safeguarding the health and safety of its workforce and has adopted a ‘Zero Harm’ objective across all operational sites. To uphold this commitment, the company undertakes comprehensive hazard identification and risk assessments tailored to specific activities, in accordance with Indian regulations and international standards such as those outlined by the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA).
Robust health and safety procedures have been established for routine, non-routine, and specialized tasks, encompassing industrial hygiene, traffic safety, chemical handling and spill control, working at heights, ladder safety, and electrical safety.
To build a culture of safety, IFGL provides structured induction and periodic training programs for all employees, including senior management. These are delivered as per an annual training calendar and supplemented by online modules to encourage self-directed learning.
Emergency preparedness is integral to IFGL’s safety framework, with site-specific action plans, trained response teams, and fire safety installations deployed across all manufacturing units. Each facility is supported by dedicated health and safety personnel and a Safety Committee responsible for monitoring key indicators and reporting to management.
Additionally, the Company conducts regular internal assessments and engages external experts to evaluate safety performance. Supplier evaluation procedures also incorporate EHS and ESG criteria to ensure responsible practices throughout the supply chain.

IFGL has implemented a robust incident reporting and investigation procedure. No major Health and Safety related incidents took places in FY 2024-25 in any of IFGL’s manufacturing facilities in India. Further, no significant risks and hazards were identified from assessments of Health & Safety Practices and working conditions. Company formulates time-bound action plan on Health and Safety deviations at workplace and IFGL has made budgetary allocations for this purpose.

IFGL recognizes that stakeholders fulfi various and interrelated roles that are crucial to the success and sustainability of its operations. Therefore, the Company prioritizes active engagement with stakeholders through effective communication to comprehend their interests, address their concerns, and fulfil their needs. This strategy fosters long-term value creation, enhances competitive advantage, and produces a beneficial impact on both society and the environment.
Stakeholders are identified through a comprehensive mapping process, and IFGL engages  a broad spectrum to gain deeper insights into their expectations and to inform its business strategies. The Company classifies its key stakeholders based on  following attributes:

•Dependency: Stakeholders who are either directly dependent on IFGL’s activities or whose actions directly impact the Company’s operations. This includes all internal stakeholders.

•Responsibility: Stakeholders for whom the Company bears legal, commercial, operational, or ethical responsibilities.

•Other: External stakeholders such as NGOs, media, and news agencies.

To support meaningful engagement, IFGL has established structured processes for periodic interaction with each stakeholder group.

IFGL manufactures and supplies refractories to customers in both domestic and international markets. The Board of Directors (BOD), through its various committees, actively gathers feedback from both internal and external stakeholders. These committees engage in ongoing, proactive dialogue to understand stakeholder concerns across a range of areas, including economic and financial performance, environmental sustainability, social responsibility, and governance matters. This approach allows IFGL to remain closely attuned to the evolving needs and expectations of its stakeholders.

IFGL actively consults with a range of stakeholders including employees, suppliers, customers, service providers, and local communities to identify and manage ESG topics that are material to its business operations. Striking a harmonious balance between business growth, sustainability, and long-term value creation remains a key priority for IFGL. Insights gained through stakeholder engagement are instrumental in shaping the Company’s ESG policies and strategic initiatives.

There are no vulnerable or marginalized stakeholders directly associated with the Company’s business operations. However, IFGL has undertaken a range of CSR initiatives through the IFGL Refractories Welfare Trust. These programs have been developed based on feedback and concerns received from key stakeholders and are focused on addressing critical issues and meeting the needs of communities surrounding its manufacturing facilities in India. IFGL’s CSR efforts include supporting schools, establishing vocational training centers for skill development, promoting health and hygiene awareness among school children, and encouraging participation in sports.

IFGL’s commitment to respecting and promoting human rights is both a moral responsibility and a key element of its corporate responsibility and sustainable business practices. The Company is dedicated to upholding human rights across its operations and supply chain and has developed and implemented a Human Rights and Grievance Redressal Policy. This policy ensures a strong focus on maintaining a harassment-free environment and a safe, healthy workplace. Grievances raised by internal or external stakeholders are addressed through a structured seven-stage resolution process, with all cases documented in a dedicated grievance register to ensure transparency and accountability.

IFGL have  'Zero Tolerance' policy towards discrimination and sexual harassment. Any concerns related to these issues are addressed  confidentially   and seriously.ly Retaliation against individuals reporting concerns in good faith is strictly prohibited. Anyone found engaged in retaliatory actions is prone to  disciplinary measures including termination. These procedures complement  company's Vigil Mechanism, Whistle-Blower Policy, and Prevention of Sexual Harassment Policy.

Human rights are a key component of IFGL’s core value of "Respecting  Rights and Dignity of All People." The company has established  internal policies and procedures to uphold human rights, leading to an exemplary track record with no history of human rights grievances or complaints.

HR department at each of IFGL's manufacturing facilities in India regularly conduct inspections to address human rights-related concerns. Additionally, the company consistently evaluates  performance of service providers engaged to identify  human rights violations.

As part of our ongoing commitment to reducing greenhouse gas emissions, the Company has undertaken multiple energy efficiency and renewable energy initiatives across its operations. These include the installation of variable frequency drives to optimize energy consumption, daylight-based operation of lighting systems in specific areas such as raw material shed, and a progressive shift towards renewable energy sources. IFGL adheres to the principles of the 3R philosophy—Reduce, Reuse, and Recycle—across all its manufacturing facilities in India. The Company is committed to minimizing the use of hazardous and toxic substances by applying a structured risk mitigation approach, including elimination, reduction, and substitution, evaluated on a case-by-case basis. Through advanced manufacturing practices, IFGL ensures operational efficiency while actively reducing waste generation.
Hazardous waste generated from manufacturing operations is managed in strict compliance with the provisions of the "Hazardous and Other Wastes (Management and Transboundary Movement) Rules, 2016." The Company secures requisite authorizations from regulatory authorities and follows the guidelines prescribed by the respective State Pollution Control Boards. Waste materials are disposed of through authorized re-processors, facilitating the recovery and reuse of valuable resources wherever feasible. Additionally, IFGL conducts regular training programs for employees and workers to promote awareness and best practices in waste minimization and safe handling.

IFGL has a Business Continuity Plan (BCP) in place, along with facility specific Emergency Response Plans (ERP). The BCP outlines potential scenarios that could disrupt business operations and includes recovery strategies. It addresses key areas such as raw material supply, use of natural resources, data and information security, regulatory compliance, and related disruptions. The ERP identifies possible emergencies, both man-made and natural, that could impact operations in the short or long term. It also defines the roles and responsibilities of on-site emergency responders, communication protocols with external agencies, and a high-level post-emergency action plan.

IFGL continues to advance its commitment to environmental sustainability through a series of proactive measures designed to reduce its ecological footprint and enhance resource efficiency across its operations. The following key initiatives have been implemented:

•	Water Re-circulation Systems: A closed-loop water circulation framework has been introduced, substantially lowering the withdrawal of fresh water. Enhancements to the Effluent Treatment Plant (ETP), including the addition of new catchpits and the installation of a Sewage Treatment Plant (STP), further support water conservation efforts.
•	Air Emission Controls: High-efficiency bag filters and Retrofit Emission Control Devices (RECD) have been installed to effectively mitigate particulate emissions and improve overall air quality.
•	Reuse of Waste Wood: Discarded wooden pallets are being repurposed for packaging applications, promoting circularity in material use and reducing solid waste generation.
•	Energy Efficiency Measures: Variable Frequency Drives (VFDs) have been deployed on motors and pumps, contributing to improved energy performance and reduced power consumption.
•	Integration of Renewable Energy: Solar lighting systems have been installed across various plant zones and administrative areas, decreasing reliance on conventional energy sources.

These initiatives underscore IFGL’s dedication to sustainable industrial practices and its ongoing efforts to operate in an environmentally responsible manner.

IFGL’s customer-centric approach views customer complaints as opportunities to enhance service quality and exceed customer expectations, creating value with every subsequent supply. Over the years, the company has refined its complaint management process to stay responsive to changing consumer expectations for acknowledgment and resolution. Any dissatisfaction with IFGL’s products or services is addressed through the SAP-based Consumer Complaint Management tool, “CRM Cruise,” ensuring complete resolution to the customer's satisfaction. The company has established a Customer Care Policy and a Customer Relationship Management system to handle queries, feedback, and grievances. Dedicated teams manage complaints and feedback, ensuring quick responses and timely resolutions. Customers can report grievances via email at ifgl.ho@ifgl.in  or by phone. Complaints are promptly investigated and resolved within 15 days, with updates provided to concerned the customer. If resolution exceeds this time frame, the customer is promptly informed of the status.
